name: Future Tense and Plans (-ecek / -acak)
summary: Talk about what will happen and what you plan to do, using the future suffix -ecek or -acak.
explanation:
To speak about the future, Turkish adds the suffix -ecek or -acak to the verb stem. Gel- (come) becomes gelecek (he will come), and bak- (look) becomes bakacak (he will look). The choice between -ecek and -acak is two-way harmony: -ecek after a front vowel, -acak after a back vowel.

When a personal ending that starts with a vowel follows, the final k softens to ğ: gelecek + im becomes geleceğim (I will come), and bakacak + ım becomes bakacağım (I will look). This softening is regular and happens every time a vowel ending attaches.

If the stem ends in a vowel, a buffer -y- appears before the suffix: bekle- becomes bekleyeceğim (I will wait), and oku- becomes okuyacağım (I will read). The full set of endings is -im, -sin, nothing, -iz, -siniz, -ler, giving geleceğim, geleceksin, gelecek, geleceğiz, geleceksiniz, gelecekler.
pattern: stem (+ buffer y) + ecek/acak + personal ending (k softens to ğ before a vowel)

examples.en: We will go to Istanbul on holiday.
What will you do in the evening?

commonMistakes: Keeping the hard k before a vowel ending, saying gelecekim instead of geleceğim.
Choosing the wrong harmony form, saying bakecek instead of bakacak.
Forgetting the buffer -y- after a vowel stem, saying bekleeceğim instead of bekleyeceğim.
Dropping the personal ending and leaving a bare gelecek when you mean 'I will come'.
Mixing the future with the -iyor present when describing a firm plan; a scheduled future event usually takes -ecek.
tip: Learn geleceğim (I will come) and bakacağım (I will look) as twin models. The k becomes ğ whenever an ending with a vowel follows, every single time.
